title: Santaloides elmeri
taxonomy: Santaloides elmeri is a species of plant in the family Santalaceae. It belongs to the order Santalales and the subclass Rosidae. Within the class Rosopsida, Santaloides elmeri is classified as a flowering plant. The plant's classification also includes the subclass Magnoliidae, which is a subcategory of the larger group Rosidae. Further classification places the plant in the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Santaloides elmeri, a plant species native to the Philippines, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. The plant is often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections. Furthermore, it is also used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal applications.

intro: Santaloides elmeri, also known as Elmer's Santalwood, is a species of plant in the family Santalaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Santaloides elmeri include santalols, santalenes, and other sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
